title: Logical Operators in Ifs 🔗
description: Combine multiple rules inside a single 'if' statement.

slides.title: Flattening the Pyramid 🚜

quizData.question: Which operator is ideal if you need ALL conditions to be true simultaneously without nesting?
quizData.explanation: The Logical AND (&&) requires every condition chained together to be true, perfectly replacing deep nesting.

slides.title: Logical OR in Ifs 💻
slides.content: See how we can catch multiple different correct answers with a single if statement using OR (||):

slides.title: A Common Beginner Mistake! ⚠️
slides.content:
## Don't Be Lazy With Code

When speaking English, we might say: "If the color is red or blue."

So beginners often try to code it like this:
```javascript
// BAD! This will always evaluate to true!
if (color === "red" || "blue") 
```

Why does that fail? Because JavaScript sees it as two separate tests:
1. Is `color === "red"`?
2. Is `"blue"` truthy? (Yes, non-empty strings are always truthy!)

**The Fix:** You must write out the full comparison on BOTH sides of the `||`:
```javascript
// GOOD!
if (color === "red" || color === "blue") 
```

quizData.question: Which is the CORRECT way to check if a number is EITHER 5 or 10?
quizData.explanation: You cannot shortcut comparisons! You must write out the full condition (variable === value) on both sides of the logical operator.
